projects.name: Furpulse built in HTML & CSS
projects.description: My goal was to create an international platform with a meaningful purpose - in this case, animal adoption. I wanted to build something useful and user-friendly, choosing a bright and hopeful aesthetic to balance out the heavy news cycle we often see in the world today.

projects.name: Beans Co. built in HTML & CSS
projects.description: This project was born from a sudden burst of inspiration. In my excitement to bring the vision to life, I prioritized the desktop layout and learned a valuable lesson about the importance of a 'mobile-first' approach. It stands as a reminder for me to slow down, plan thoroughly, and always consider the responsive experience from the very first line of code.

projects.name: Nilsbergs Pizzeria in JavaScript
projects.description: This project focuses on interactive UI through a custom-built ordering bot. I replaced the original interface with a themed pizza background to create a more immersive and appetizing experience. It was a great exercise in using CSS to transform a basic template into a brand-specific tool, focusing on how visual cues can enhance the ordering process.

projects.name: Zen Chi built in HTML, CSS & Javascript
projects.description: Zen Chi was a project focused on finding the balance between clean, organized code and a calm, minimalist aesthetic. It served as my introduction to CSS Grid and Flexbox, a challenging but rewarding learning curve. This project highlights my ability to implement complex layouts while maintaining a serene and clutter-free user interface

projects.name: Weather app built in CSS, JS & APIs
projects.description: While the initial project focused on static weather data for Stockholm, I chose to expand the functionality by integrating the Geolocation API. This allows the app to automatically detect the user's current position to provide a personalized, real-time forecast for their specific location. It was a great challenge in handling asynchronous data and API integration.
